turns.user_message: I made myself a fresh cup of coffee this morning and just stared at it until it went completely cold. It used to be the one part of my day that actually felt good, but now it just tastes like hot water. I keep trying to force myself to enjoy the little things I used to love, but the spark just isn't there anymore. This has been happening for months now and I am honestly just tired of pretending everything is fine.

I keep coming back to that exact moment with the cold mug, wondering why the simple act of waking up feels so exhausting now. What started as a strange blip has stretched into this endless gray stretch where nothing really lands with me anymore. I try to recreate that quiet morning ritual hoping the old comfort will suddenly return, but my mind just stays completely blank. It is like I am just going through the physical steps while my actual feelings are packed away somewhere I cannot reach.

I still think about that untouched cup sitting on the counter and how it perfectly captures this heavy numbness that refuses to lift. The longer this goes on, the more I realize I am completely disconnected from anything that is supposed to matter to me. I wait for a sudden wave of excitement or even just a flicker of interest to wash over me, but the silence just keeps getting louder. I am just existing in this flat space where nothing touches me and I do not know how to break through it.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This has been going on for a while.
